description:
An aeronautical engineering student needs a conceptual OpenVSP model of the Rutan Boomerang (an asymmetric twin-fuselage aircraft). OpenVSP defaults to bilateral (XZ) symmetry, which you must explicitly disable for the offset boom and the asymmetric wing.

Read the specification file at /home/ga/Desktop/rutan_boomerang_spec.txt for dimensions.

In OpenVSP, create a new model containing:
1. A 'MainFuselage' (Pod/Fuselage) centered at the origin.
2. A 'LeftBoom' (Pod/Fuselage) offset to the left. You MUST disable XZ symmetry for this component so it doesn't mirror to the right side.
3. A 'MainWing' (Wing) connecting them and extending outward. You MUST disable XZ symmetry for this wing. Configure the Right side with 1 section and the Left side with 2 sections according to the spec.

Save the model to /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/asymmetric_concept.vsp3.
